key_insight: Pre-existing natural fractures reshape local shear stress and strain, and that redistributed stress field determines whether a hydraulic fracture is deflected, attracted, or linked.
engineering_relevance: Understanding NF-induced stress redistribution helps predict and control fracture paths during subsurface stimulation and storage, improving treatment design in naturally fractured rock.
method: Coupled PMMA laboratory fracture experiments under plane-strain loading
Digital image correlation to measure full-field displacement and strain evolution
Poroelastic extended finite element simulations with mixed-mode fracture criteria
evidence_signal: The study combines laboratory observations and numerical reproduction of fracture curvature in intact and pre-fractured specimens under fixed-base, lateral confinement, and vertical compression conditions.
watch_item: Results are based on PMMA specimens and idealized boundary conditions, so field-scale rock heterogeneity and in-situ stress complexity may limit direct transferability.
paper_title: Local Stress Redistribution Controls Interactions between Hydraulic Fractures and Pre-existing Fractures
